subject: Re: CFT fitting range setting for batch pre-processing in Warp
body:
Hi Xingyi,

I'm hoping if there's any more information you could provide? Like what 
kind of pixel size you're working with, the defoci range, and the desired 
resolution?

Yours sincerely,
Linhua Tai

On Thursday, February 24, 2022 at 6:30:21 AM UTC-5 xingyichen...@gmail.com 
wrote:

> Hey Lorenzo,
>
> Thank you for your suggestions! We are exactly grouping tilt series based 
> on defocus and fitting CTF after the first peak atm, we were wondering if 
> there is anything else that could also be taken into consideration to 
> ensure a good range for bulk pre-processing. Any suggestion is greatly 
> appreciated!
>
> Have a great day!
>
> Cheers,
>
> Cyan Ching
>
> PhD student
>
> The Physical Chemistry Curie Lab
>
> On Thu, 24 Feb 2022 at 10:23, Lorenzo Gaifas <lorenzo...@gmail.com> wrote:
>
>> Hi Cyan,
>>
>> I've had good results without grouping tilt series, and the varying 
>> defocus was handled correctly (assuming that this is a single-collection 
>> dataset; if your defoci differ a lot, I guess splitting the dataset is a 
>> better idea).
>> If you're having troubles with CTF not fitting well, make sure you skip 
>> the first CTF peak (even for the lesser defocused tilt series); I've been 
>> bitten by this several times...
>> [image: image.png]
>>
>> Good luck!
>>
>> Cheers,
>> Lorenzo
>>
>> On Wed, Feb 23, 2022 at 4:21 PM Cyan Ching <xingyichen...@gmail.com> 
>> wrote:
>>
>>> Dear Warp/M users,
>>>
>>> Greetings! 
>>>
>>> How do you set a single CTF fitting range for batch pre-processing in 
>>> Warp? Do you group tomograms based on their defocus? Any tips you'd like to 
>>> share? 
>>>
>>> Thank you!
>>>
>>> Best regards,
>>>
>>> Cyan Ching
>>>
>>> PhD student
>>>
>>> The Physical Chemistry Curie Lab
>>>
>>> --

>>> .
>>>
>>
